clinical_significance: Primary bacterial infection fighter; most common WBC
Adaptive immunity; elevated in viral infections
Least common WBC; involved in allergic reactions
CRITICAL — immature cell; hallmark of leukemia
Elongated RBC; hereditary elliptocytosis / iron deficiency
Spherical RBC; hereditary spherocytosis / autoimmune hemolysis
Crescent RBC; sickle cell disease (HbS mutation)
RBC with central dense area; thalassemia / liver disease
Heavy dark granules; severe bacterial sepsis
Ruptured lymphocyte; CLL hallmark on smear
Preparation/staining artefact; should be excluded from analysis
